svg.title: Planck spectra at three temperatures (Wien's law)
svg.desc: A family of Planck blackbody curves at 4000, 5000, and 6000 K. As temperature rises the peak grows and shifts to higher frequency; a dashed locus traces the peak, illustrating Wien's law that the peak frequency is proportional to temperature.

cajal:figure.book: Quantum Mechanics — Foundations: The Quantum World
cajal:figure.figure-title: Planck spectra at three temperatures (Wien's law)
cajal:figure.author: Humanitarians AI Incorporated

svg.text: Blackbody spectra: the peak shifts with temperature
